PRSG Lab

Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Geoinformatics Lab

Welcome to the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Geoinformatics (PRSG) Lab.

The PRSG Lab is a geospatial research group in the Department of Civil Engineering at Jeonbuk National University. Our research focuses on precise geospatial data generation, Earth observation data analysis, and three-dimensional spatial modeling based on the principles of geodesy, photogrammetry, remote sensing, and imaging geometry. The lab works at the intersection of photogrammetric measurement, satellite and aerial remote sensing, LiDAR point cloud processing, geographic information science, and 3D reconstruction. We develop and apply methods for extracting reliable spatial information from images, point clouds, and other geospatial observations, with particular emphasis on sensor geometry, spatial accuracy, data integration, and quantitative analysis. Our research spans a wide range of platforms and scales, including satellite imagery, UAV mapping, terrestrial laser scanning, mobile mapping, and close-range imaging systems. Through these data sources, we address problems related to precise mapping, terrain and object modeling, environmental monitoring, infrastructure analysis, and smart geospatial information systems.

By combining rigorous geometric modeling with modern computational approaches, the PRSG Lab aims to produce accurate, interpretable, and practically useful geospatial information for scientific research, engineering applications, and decision-making in both built and natural environments.

Research Keywords

  • Photogrammetry
  • Remote sensing
  • Satellite image geometry
  • UAV mapping
  • Pointcloudmetry
  • 3D reconstruction
